source.title: Equipotentiality of thalamo-amygdala and thalamo-cortico-amygdala circuits in auditory fear conditioning

confidence_rationale: Romanski & LeDoux provide the definitive experimental demonstration of the parallel low-road/high-road architecture. Selective lesions show that either the thalamo-amygdala pathway alone OR the thalamo-cortico-amygdala pathway alone is sufficient for fear conditioning; only destroying both disrupts conditioning. This directly grounds ARC-027's claim that the HARM stream operates via a parallel thalamic pathway that bypasses cortical (E1) processing: the sensory signal reaches the amygdala-equivalent independently of the cortical route. The experiment was conducted with auditory stimuli but the same circuit architecture applies to nociceptive/threat signals via the spinothalamic tract feeding posterior thalamic nuclei (VPM/Po) which project directly to the lateral amygdala.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Either the thalamo-amygdala projection (fast, coarse sensory signal direct to lateral amygdala) or the thalamo-cortico-amygdala projection (slower, more refined signal via sensory cortex) is individually sufficient for fear conditioning. Both must be destroyed to disrupt conditioning. The two pathways are equipotential and parallel — they carry the same stimulus information via different routes and timescales.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-027: the HARM stream must be implemented as a parallel pathway with its own thalamic input that bypasses E1 (cortical) processing. The biological substrate — demonstrated here for auditory fear — generalises to nociception via the spinothalamic → posterior thalamic → lateral amygdala route. In REE terms: harm detection does not depend on z_world being formed by E1; it operates on a dedicated channel that converges with E3 at the commitment gate. This is why treating harm_eval as a head on z_world fails: E1 processes the WORLD stream (thalamo-cortico pathway), but the HARM stream takes the thalamo-amygdala route in parallel.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The original experiment used auditory conditioned stimuli, not nociception directly. The nociceptive low-road is anatomically distinct (spinothalamic → VPM/Po → lateral amygdala vs auditory thalamus → lateral amygdala) but follows the same architectural logic. Transfer to REE's digital substrate requires treating 'harm input features' as a separate channel, not re-using E1's world model outputs.
mapping.source_context: Classic 1992 paper establishing the low-road/high-road framework experimentally. Uses selective lesion methodology with simultaneous cardiovascular and freezing behavioral readouts. Foundational to LeDoux's fear circuit work and directly cited in 'The Emotional Brain' (LeDoux 1996).

confidence_components.notes: Landmark paper with very high source quality. Mapping fidelity is strong — the parallel pathway architecture is the direct claim. Transfer risk is modest: experiment uses auditory CS not nociception, but the posterior thalamic → amygdala nociceptive pathway is well-established by other work (e.g. Bernard & Besson, Gauriau & Bernard).
